The filing shows Berkshire held $1.02 billion worth of tech giant Google shares and $2.6 billion worth of Delta Air Lines shares at the end of the first quarter.
The firm also held several Google positions, including one worth about $11.87 billion, bringing its total reported Alphabet exposure far higher.
Its Alphabet Class A holdings included 41,283,098 shares in its largest reported line item.
The filings show the firm divested its positions in Amazon, Visa, Mastercard and UnitedHealth, while also selling out of several other holdings, such as stakes in the Atlanta Braves, a professional Major League Baseball team, and Domino‚Äôs pizza.

Berkshire dumped ex-Buffett associate's portfolio, WSJ learns ‚Äì Oninvest","content":"Todd Combs and remaining Berkshire investment manager Ted Weschler managed a total of about 10% of the portfolio, with Warren Buffett managing the remaining 90% / Photo: Andriy Blokhin/Shutterstock.com
Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has liquidated positions in the portfolio for which investment manager Todd Combs was responsible, who will move to a competitor at the end of 2025.
The new head of Berkshire Greg Abel, who will succeed Buffett in January 2026, has sold a stake in the company, which until his departure from the company at the end of 2025 was managed by Todd Combs, reported The Wall Street Journal.
Before his firing, Combs managed about 5% of Berkshire Hathaway's entire equity portfolio, which has a combined value of $300 billion, so the "oracle of Omaha" investment firm may have sold about $15 billion worth of stock in the first quarter of this year, Barron's calculated.
The sale of Constellation Brands and Pool shares in the same quarter also hints that Combs was in charge of them.
2 as part of its first-quarter financial report on Form 10-Q.
More detailed information will be available in mid-Ma: the holding company, along with other large institutional investors, will disclose Form 13-F, which will include all transactions in specific securities from January through March.
Combs, who had long served as one of Buffett's chief investment managers, left Berkshire in December 2025 for a senior position at investment bank JPMorgan Chase."}]}

# Todd Combs Leaves Berkshire Hathaway for JPMorgan Chase as Buffett Nears Retirement - The New York Times. DealBook|Todd Combs, a Warren Buffett Prot√©g√© at Berkshire, Jumps to JPMorgan. https://www.nytimes.com/2025/12/08/business/dealbook/berkshire-todd-combs-jpmorgan.html. # Todd Combs, a Warren Buffett Prot√©g√© at Berkshire, Jumps to JPMorgan. The departure of the senior stock picker was one of several leadership changes ahead of Mr. Buffett‚Äôs planned departure as chief executive of Berkshire Hathaway. Todd Combs joined Berkshire Hathaway in 2010 to become one of the managers of the trillion-dollar conglomerate‚Äôs vast stock portfolio.Credit...Brian Losness/Reuters. Berkshire Hathaway, the trillion-dollar conglomerate, announced a shuffling of its leadership ranks on Monday, shortly before Warren E. Combs, one of Mr. Buffett‚Äôs chief stock-picking lieutenants. Mr. Combs, who is also the chief executive of Berkshire‚Äôs GEICO auto insurance business, will join JPMorgan Chase to lead a recently formed investment initiative. The moves were announced as Berkshire prepares for a post-Buffett era. Mr. Buffett has run Berkshire for 60 years, becoming a household name by transforming the company into a sprawling group with a vast portfolio defined by his choices. Last month, Mr. Buffett wrote in a letter to the company‚Äôs shareholders that he would no longer send Berkshire‚Äôs annual investor letter, ending a decades-long tradition that helped him win millions of fans. Now, one of the two men whom Mr. Buffett had picked to help manage Berkshire‚Äôs investments is going, too. In 2010, Mr. Buffett plucked Mr. Combs out of relative obscurity at a small hedge fund to become one of Berkshire‚Äôs investment managers. That put Mr. Combs ‚Äî and a later colleague, Ted Weschler ‚Äî in charge of a vast stock portfolio that helped power the conglomerate‚Äôs financial success. Mr. Combs also became the leader of GEICO, which is among Berkshire‚Äôs largest wholly owned businesses. Subscribe to The Times to read as many articles as you like. To support this initiative, JPMorgan has established a new council that includes notable figures such as Amazon founder Jeff Bezos and Ford CEO Jim Farley. Combs, aged 54 and a former hedge fund manager, has an existing relationship with JPMorgan, having joined the bank‚Äôs board in 2016. His appointment follows his involvement in the healthcare venture launched by JPMorgan, Amazon, and Berkshire in 2018, although that project was discontinued after three years. Buffett praised the Combs hire, stating, ‚ÄúJPMorgan, as usually is the case, has made a good decision.‚Äù.
